reasoning: The 30-day average of fatalities over the preceding 360 days is approximately 1.417. Since fatalities are discrete, 2 or more fatalities are required in a given 30-day window to exceed this average and resolve YES. For the first resolution date (Mar 23), the 30-day window is Feb 21 - Mar 23. A comprehensive search found no political violence fatalities in Germany between Feb 21 and Mar 16. With only 7 days remaining in this window, the probability of reaching 2+ fatalities is very low (~4%). For the subsequent full 30-day windows, the base rate of observing 2+ ACLED fatalities in a month in Germany is estimated around 25-30%, given that the ~17 annual fatalities tend to cluster in a few high-profile incidents or monthly bounds. I assign a baseline of ~27% for April 15, and steadily regress my probabilities toward 0.5 for the later dates to account for increasing uncertainty over time.
